title: New boxset of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: New boxset of Evangelion.             Quote Post by toReason » Sat Apr 28, 2007 6:40 am I recieved an e-mail from cdjapan today/this morning about a new limited edition boxset of Eva due out this august. [url]http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KIBA-91401[/url] What I found of particular interest is some info in the blurb : and bonus disc with creditless intros and outros, advanced video, advanced video for the upcoming new theatrical feature, and footage from the upcoming live-action film.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 28, 2007 7:33 am Someone probably goofed up the translation. It might either be the Alternate Live Action Sequence or the videos that ADV included in Ressurection and Genesis Reborn. I doubt they've kept filming of Eva live action a secret. They haven't formally announced a director or anything as of yet.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Apr 28, 2007 9:43 am I kind of hold out small hope that they have new concept art or whatever for live action.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Apr 28, 2007 9:56 am It's just another limited edition release to cash in on the people who weren't able to get the previous limited edition release. Although, I did read some complaints that the first box was a little flimsy, so perhaps they will at least correct that aspect.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 28, 2007 10:01 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Someone probably goofed up the translation. It might either be the Alternate Live Action Sequence or the videos that ADV included in Ressurection and Genesis Reborn.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sat Apr 28, 2007 10:41 am Is there a european or american box set with progressive footage? Mine's the american Platinum Thinpack, and while I'm content with the content, I'd pay for a new one if it wasn't interlaced.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 28, 2007 10:43 am Kaysow wrote: Is there a european or american box set with progressive footage?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sat Apr 28, 2007 12:44 pm Feared as much. + it's not like they tell us stuff like that in the product description. Only progressive box I've got is Elfen Lied.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Apr 28, 2007 1:01 pm Kaysow wrote: Feared as much. + it's not like they tell us stuff like that in the product description. Only progressive box I've got is Elfen Lied.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sat Apr 28, 2007 2:20 pm Oh I have seen interlacing on R2 DVDs. It's not just Japan that uses it. It's impossible not to be bothered by it when you rip DVDs and make AMVs. For turning 23fps into 29fps without making things look choppy, I guess it's a useful technique. But it's a massacre. So there's no code to identify progressive? Bummer, but not unexpected. At least big, expensive restorations seem to bring a change in that. Akira got one, and it's not interlaced. Neither are any of Disney's special edition DVDs.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Apr 28, 2007 6:58 pm BrikHaus wrote: It's just another limited edition release to cash in on the people who weren't able to get the previous limited edition release. <snip>

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 28, 2007 7:59 pm There are TWO releases of this: Somebody named E from EML wrote: IIRC, there were two releases of this version, one by King Records, and one by GAiNAX. > 2) The Second Impact DVD Box Sets collecting the > series as it aired on > TV-Tokyo > 3) The Renewal of Evangelion DVD set > 4) This upcoming set. Actually, it seems there are two flavours of the upcoming set: Evangelion DVD box '07 edition http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=1404 29,400 yen To be released 1 August 2007 Evangelion DVD box Reissue edition with reference to new 5.1ch sound and new telecine from component digital new master http://eva.yahoo.co.jp/gentei/dvdbox.html 41,790 yen To be released at the end of August 2007 (Sounds like an opportunity for ADV to re-release Evangelion yet again :P ) E

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sat Apr 28, 2007 9:32 pm This looks like the old renewal red boxset. Will this one also bring the books that one had?

[]:             Quote Post by Pimp Gendo » Sun Apr 29, 2007 2:40 am Kaysow wrote: Is there a european or american box set with progressive footage? Mine's the american Platinum Thinpack, and while I'm content with the content, I'd pay for a new one if it wasn't interlaced.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sun Apr 29, 2007 6:55 am Yeah, in actuality, it's not like I mind buying new boxes that much. As long as there's significant improvement to warrant it's release.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Apr 29, 2007 9:44 am AchtungAffen wrote: This looks like the old renewal red boxset. Will this one also bring the books that one had?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 29, 2007 3:11 pm .....Episode 16 was NOT remastered??? No wonder the coloring looked so white/faded at the start of the episode! :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 29, 2007 3:32 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: .....Episode 16 was NOT remastered???

[]:             Quote Post by tian2992 » Wed Sep 19, 2007 10:39 am HD would Rule....

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Wed Sep 19, 2007 12:01 pm tian2992 wrote: HD would Rule....
